content: Find where next_batch_end is defined and referenced. Use file_search and grep_search first. Return bullet list path:line.

Here are all locations where `next_batch_end` is defined and referenced:

- crates/openfang-runtime/src/agent_loop.rs:66 — definition: `fn next_batch_end(deduped_calls: &[&ToolCall], start: usize) -> usize {`
- crates/openfang-runtime/src/agent_loop.rs:731 — reference: `let batch_end = next_batch_end(&deduped_calls, call_idx);`
- crates/openfang-runtime/src/agent_loop.rs:1912 — reference: `let batch_end = next_batch_end(&deduped_calls, call_idx);`
- crates/openfang-runtime/src/agent_loop.rs:3148 — test function: `fn test_next_batch_end_groups_read_only_and_splits_mutating() {`
- crates/openfang-runtime/src/agent_loop.rs:3172 — reference in test: `assert_eq!(next_batch_end(&deduped, 0), 2, "two read-only calls batch");`
- crates/openfang-runtime/src/agent_loop.rs:3173 — reference in test: `assert_eq!(next_batch_end(&deduped, 2), 3, "mutating call stays single");`
- crates/openfang-runtime/src/agent_loop.rs:3174 — reference in test: `assert_eq!(next_batch_end(&deduped, 3), 4, "trailing read-only single call");`

All references and the definition are in `crates/openfang-runtime/src/agent_loop.rs`.

content:
Here are the main HTTP API routes defined in your project (from crates/openfang-api/src/routes.rs):

### Agent Routes
- `POST /api/agents` — Spawn a new agent
- `GET /api/agents` — List all agents
- `POST /api/agents/:id/message` — Send a message to an agent
- `GET /api/agents/:id/session` — Get agent session (conversation history)
- `DELETE /api/agents/:id` — Kill an agent
- `POST /api/agents/{id}/restart` — Restart a crashed/stuck agent
- `GET /api/agents/:id` — Get a single agent's detailed info
- `POST /api/agents/:id/message/stream` — SSE streaming response

### Workflow Routes
- `POST /api/workflows` — Register a new workflow
- `GET /api/workflows` — List all workflows
- `POST /api/workflows/:id/run` — Execute a workflow
- `GET /api/workflows/:id/runs` — List runs for a workflow
- `GET /api/workflows/:id` — Get a single workflow by ID
- `PUT /api/workflows/:id` — Update a workflow definition
- `DELETE /api/workflows/:id` — Delete a workflow definition

### Trigger Routes
- `POST /api/triggers` — Register a new event trigger
- `GET /api/triggers` — List all triggers
- `DELETE /api/triggers/:id` — Remove a trigger

### Channel Routes
- `GET /api/channels` — List all channel adapters
- `POST /api/channels/{name}/configure` — Save channel secrets + config fields
- `DELETE /api/channels/{name}/configure` — Remove channel secrets + config section
- `POST /api/channels/{name}/test` — Connectivity check + optional live test message
- `POST /api/channels/reload` — Manually trigger a channel hot-reload

### Template Routes
- `GET /api/templates` — List available agent templates
- `GET /api/templates/:name` — Get template details

### Skills, Hands, Integrations, Models, Providers, Sessions, Memory, Budget, Cron, Audit, Comms, Pairing, and more
- There are many additional routes for skills, hands, integrations, models, providers, sessions, memory, budget, cron jobs, audit logs, inter-agent comms, device pairing, and more.

**Note:** This is a high-level summary. The file contains a comprehensive API for agent management, workflows, triggers, channels, skills, and system operations. If you want a specific category (e.g., only workflows or only channels), let me know!
